notes.note: Publius Licinius Crassus, younger son of the triumvir; served as cavalry commander under Caesar in Gaul, then died in the cavalry charge that broke at Carrhae in June 53 BC.
Caesar's eight-month campaign at Alexandria in pursuit of Pompey's killers (autumn 48 to spring 47 BC), with the dynastic intervention on behalf of Cleopatra against her brother Ptolemy XIII.
Caesar was in Spain from the end of 46 BC for the Munda campaign, which closed the surviving Pompeian resistance in March 45 BC.

The Stoic philosopher who lived in Cicero's house from his youth until Diodotus's death in 60 BC, blind in his last years; the teacher whose lessons in dialectic Cicero refers back to in the philosophical works.

Hellenistic kings and Roman dynasts cultivated Greek-language historical celebrants; Apollonius's project would have been a prose history in Greek, in the tradition of Theophanes of Mytilene's life of Pompey.
